programme.title: Bholaa
programme.desc: After a decade-long prison sentence, an ex-convict endeavours to meet his daughter. However, he faces numerous obstacles during his journey.

programme.title: Khiladi 786
programme.desc: The underachieving son of a marriage bureau owner attempts to marry a don's daughter to a supposed police officer in an effort to prove his worth to his father.

programme.title: Judaai
programme.desc: A greedy wife sells her husband for wealth, but soon discovers that a luxurious life demands a heavy, costly price she didn't anticipate.

programme.title: Manikarnika
programme.desc: Manikarnika, The Wife Of The King Of Jhansi, Refuses To Bow Down When The East India Company Tries To Annex The Kingdom. Her Rebellion Soon Turns Into A Fiery Revolution Against The British Raj.

programme.title: Sooryavanshi
programme.desc: With a terrorist attack looming over Mumbai, ATS chief Sooryavanshi races against time to find 600 kgs of missing RDX and the terrorists plotting the city's destruction.

programme.title: Tumbbad
programme.desc: A revered goddess is angered when humans build a temple for her child, triggering divine punishment and revealing the consequences of mortal hubris and sacred boundaries.

programme.title: Farrey
programme.desc: Niyati, a brilliant orphan, wins a scholarship to a prestigious school but finds herself caught in a cheating scandal after aiding her wealthy but struggling classmate.

programme.title: Faraaz
programme.desc: Police respond swiftly to a hostage situation at a Bangladesh restaurant, confronting Islamic terrorists in a high-stakes rescue operation.

programme.title: Ashgrove
programme.desc: In the near future, scientist Dr. Jennifer Ashgrove fights a water crisis. Retreating with her husband, their strained marriage becomes humanity's hope or doom.

programme.title: Mr. & Mrs. Mahi
programme.desc: Mahendra, a failed cricketer, and Mahima, a doctor, bond over cricket in their arranged marriage, with Mahendra coaching Mahima to pursue her cricket dreams.

programme.title: Sye Raa Narasimha Reddy
programme.desc: A historical action epic inspired by the life of Uyyalawada Narasimha Reddy, who revolted against the atrocities of East India Company 10 years before the Sepoy Mutiny.

programme.title: Bhairavakona
programme.desc: A man ventures into the mysterious Bhairavakona, seeking answers from a place where escape seems impossible, unraveling secrets buried deep.

programme.title: Pardes
programme.desc: Kishorilal settled in USA comes down to India proposes that his son Rajeev marry Kusum, his friend's daughter.

programme.title: Shivam
programme.desc: Shiva is the ideal friend who helps lovers unite. When he falls head over heels in love with Tanu, he has to fight Bhoji Reddy while keeping Abhi, who kidnaps Tanu, at bay.

programme.title: Hero No. 1
programme.desc: Timid Rajesh flees a tyrant father, finds Meena abroad, and plans marriage. When Meena's parents object, Rajesh woos them in the quirkiest possible way.

programme.title: Deva
programme.desc: Dev Ambre, a ruthless cop, loses his memory after solving a murder. He must reinvestigate, hidden from everyone except DCP Farhan Khan during dangerous investigations.

programme.title: Boonie Bears: Back To Earth
programme.desc: Bramble gains powers from a fallen alien object, but an invader wants it back. He teams up with Vick and the bears to fight back.

programme.title: Kismat Konnection
programme.desc: Raj and Priya's lives intertwine through fate, leading to love, challenges, and destiny-driven twists in this romantic drama about luck and relationships.

programme.title: Naam Shabana
programme.desc: After serving time for killing her abusive father, Shabana is recruited by an Indian spy agency for a highly classified mission.

programme.title: No Entry
programme.desc: A married man decides to have an extramarital affair. Things do not go as planned and life becomes a bundle of confusion.

programme.title: Spyder
programme.desc: An intelligence officer battles a deranged serial killer who murders just to hear loved ones cry, racing to save the city before more lives are heartbreakingly lost.

programme.title: Indian
programme.desc: DCP Raj Shekhar Azad nabs terrorist Wasim Khan, but uncovers deep police corruption involving his father-in-law. Framed for murder, he must clear his name and fight for justice.

programme.title: Shiva The Super Hero 3
programme.desc: Three friends invest in a beach resort that turns haunted. They enlist Rudra, a renowned mentalist, to uncover the paranormal mystery and restore peace.

programme.title: Ramaiya Vastavaiya
programme.desc: City boy Ram falls for village girl Sona, raised by her protective brother Raghuveer. When Ram's mother insults Sona, Raghuveer challenges Ram to prove he deserves to marry her.

programme.title: Krack
programme.desc: Three notorious criminals clash with Potharaju Shankar, a hot-tempered cop, in a series of intense confrontations that ultimately lead them behind bars.

programme.title: Fitoor
programme.desc: A young Kashmiri boy Noor tries to win the heart of his muse Firdaus , while her mother hatches a scheme to end his quest for true love.

programme.title: Zid
programme.desc: Maya falls in love with Ronnie, a crime reporter, and gets obsessed with him. Things take a turn for the worse when they both get involved in a murder case.

programme.title: Tenali Ramakrishna B.A.B.L
programme.desc: Money-minded Tenali Ramakrishna, shifts to pursue justice in a criminal case.

programme.title: Mr Zoo Keeper
programme.desc: A zookeeper forges a deep, hopeful bond with the remaining animals and birds in the zoo, learning responsibility, empathy, and resilience through daily care and quiet companionship.

programme.title: Rock On 2
programme.desc: Old friends reunite to revive their music band, confronting unresolved issues and personal struggles as they seek redemption through melodies.

programme.title: Dhadak
programme.desc: Young lovers from different social strata defy family and tradition to be together, leading to a tragic journey that challenges their innocence and emotional resolve.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike
programme.desc: India's military retaliates against a terrorist attack with surgical precision, showcasing valor, strategy, and patriotic spirit in a gripping narrative.

programme.title: Firaaq
programme.desc: After the 2002 Gujarat riots, survivors, perpetrators, and silent witnesses face lasting scars, forcing them to confront the way tragedy reshapes lives, hearts, and entire communities forever.

programme.title: Mr. & Mrs. Mahi
programme.desc: A former cricketer turned coach, played by Rajkummar Rao, helps his wife, Janhvi Kapoor, achieve stardom in the sport, but struggles to cope with her rising fame and his own fading glory.

programme.title: Yudhra
programme.desc: Yudhra seeks revenge on a cartel for his parents' murder. He infiltrates the group, but uncovers a shocking truth about his own history. This discovery challenges his resolve.

programme.title: English vinglish
programme.desc: Housewife And Caterer Shashi Is Usually Mocked By Her Family For Not Knowing English. Her Attempt To Learn The Language Helps Her Rediscover Herself And Reassert Her Value As A Mother And A Wife.

programme.title: Welcome To New York
programme.desc: An unexpected trip to New York City sets a duo off on a comic adventure that changes their lives forever.

programme.title: Sniff
programme.desc: An anosmic boy gains super smell powers and uses them to fight crime, turning into a pint-sized superhero with humor and noble purpose.

programme.title: Lost
programme.desc: When a young activist disappears under mysterious circumstances, a journalist embarks on a journey to unravel the truth behind the mystery.

programme.title: Saand Ki Aankh
programme.desc: Two elderly women become sharpshooting champions, defying age and gender norms to inspire a generation through grit and determination.

programme.title: Go Goa Gone
programme.desc: Three slackers face chaos on a zombie-infested island, blending horror and hilarity as they fight the undead with absurd tactics and plenty of dark humor.

programme.title: Manikarnika
programme.desc: Manikarnika, the wife of the king of Jhansi, refuses to bow down when the East India Company tries to annex the kingdom. Her rebellion soon turns into a fiery revolution against the British Raj.

programme.title: Rustom
programme.desc: A decorated naval officer kills his wife's lover, sparking a gripping courtroom drama filled with patriotism, betrayal, and hidden motives.

programme.title: Dobaaraa
programme.desc: A woman saves the life of a 12-year old boy who earlier died due to a thunderstorm 25 years ago, by connecting through a TV channel. But, this incident changes her present day life.

programme.title: Fitoor
programme.desc: Set in Kashmir, a haunting romance blooms between a passionate artist and a mysterious heiress, shaped by class divide, longing, and painful revelations.

programme.title: Dunki
programme.desc: A group of friends set out on a perilous journey to emigrate to the United Kingdom via a clandestine route called 'donkey flight', risking their lives in the process.

programme.title: Nill Battey Sannata
programme.desc: Apu, an unmotivated student, overcomes math struggles with her determined mother Chanda. Their shared journey inspires Apu to pursue IAS dreams.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah takes a look at mining, touring from Cornwall to Scotland to explore how tin, slate, lead and coal were all extracted from the ground. Along the way, he visits old mine workings, drives and winding engines, and he descends a mine shaft strapped to the top of the cage.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boat life with Robbie Cumming. Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal - will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: Documentary-maker David Malone delves into the secrets of ocean waves. In an elegant and original film, he finds that waves are not made of water, that some waves travel sideways, and that the sound of the ocean comes not from water but from bubbles. Waves are not only beautiful but also profoundly important, and there is a surprising connection between the life cycle of waves and the life of human beings.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah visits the North East, which is rich in railway history. At Bowes Railway, he sees an early engineering project by George Stephenson, which was a stationary engine that pulled coal wagons uphill with a rope. At Darlington Railway Museum he admires Stephenson's original Locomotive No 1, the first to run from Darlington to Stockton. At the National Railway Museum, York, he rides on a replica of the Rocket, made by Stephenson's son Robert, and at Ffestiniog Railway, he sees how a new locomotive is designed with computer aids and rides on the footplate and stokes the boiler of a Black Five at Llangollen Railway.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. There are engine issues and a leaky boat to fix before Robbie reaches Liverpool Docks.

programme.title: What Are UFOs?
programme.desc: For decades, UFOs have intrigued the world, but many scientists avoided studying them. In the United States, after highly publicised sightings of unidentified objects by US Navy pilots, UFOs are moving out of the shadows and into the light as NASA pledges to study them scientifically. So what does science have to say? Though some are identified as balloons, drones, weather phenomena or optical illusions, others remain mysterious. Could they be the result of secret new technology? And what would it take for alien engineers to traverse vast distances to send probes or visit Earth from other solar systems? Explore the evidence as astrophysicists and engineers use new technologies to investigate the strangest objects in our skies.

programme.title: Talking Art: Cezanne
programme.desc: Celebrities talk about which of Cezanne's works they find most inspiring.

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: In the second episode, our cameras pick up and follow the projects’ next major construction milestones. We get up close and personal with the world’s largest land-based crane during the biggest lift on the project to date. We learn how the team has created an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from the worst imaginable weather events - including a once-in-10,000 year storm surge - to avoid a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. We also follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded World War II bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Warren falls in love, but when his romance turns sour he makes the decision not to live a lie. Meanwhile, Milly and O'Donnell are becoming increasingly close at work, and Jerry finds an unexpected ally in Anna.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: O'Donnell could prove to be Milly's knight in shining armour, but will she risk her relationship with Egg for a romantic fantasy? Meanwhile, Anna decides that an older man is just what she needs, and Miles deals with a flasher who claims to be a nudist.

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston investigates the forensic advances that have elevated the murder weapon from its role of mere evidence to that of key witness.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. There are engine issues and a leaky boat to fix before Robbie reaches Liverpool Docks.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah visits the North East, which is rich in railway history. At Bowes Railway, he sees an early engineering project by George Stephenson, which was a stationary engine that pulled coal wagons uphill with a rope. At Darlington Railway Museum he admires Stephenson's original Locomotive No 1, the first to run from Darlington to Stockton. At the National Railway Museum, York, he rides on a replica of the Rocket, made by Stephenson's son Robert, and at Ffestiniog Railway, he sees how a new locomotive is designed with computer aids and rides on the footplate and stokes the boiler of a Black Five at Llangollen Railway.

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station

We get up close and personal with the world’s largest land-based crane during the biggest lift on the project to date. We also follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded World War II bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah examines the skill of the shipbuilders and machine engineers who turned Britain into a great manufacturing nation. In Bristol, Fred visits the SS Great Britain and pays tribute to the designer and his hero, Isambard Kingdom Brunel. Fred also travels to Scotland to take a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley. Back in England, he visits the Windermere Steamboat Museum, the Long Shop Museum in Leiston, Suffolk, and the Kew Bridge Steam Museum. Plus, Fred drives his pride and joy, the Aveling & Porter steam roller, talking about its history and recalling one rather dramatic crash he had while driving it.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores London, the ultimate 'city of villages'. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expanding capital, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage. And in recent years, Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow.

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: As good as any Dickens novel, this is the triumphant and tragic story of the greatest architectural dynasty of the 19th century. Dan Cruickshank charts the rise of Sir George Gilbert Scott to the very heights of success, the fall of his son George Junior and the rise again of his grandson Giles. It is a story of architects bent on a mission to rebuild Britain. From the Romantic heights of the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station to the modern image of Bankside power station (now Tate Modern), this is the story of a family that shaped the Victorian age and left a giant legacy.

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Two unlikely partners in a bounty-hunting scam join forces in a new venture as they try to outdo a sadistic criminal looking for the $20,000 that a soldier has buried somewhere in the desert.

programme.title: Clint Eastwood: The Man with No Name
programme.desc: Profile of Clint Eastwood, the iconic actor and director, in which he talks to Iain Johnstone. Featuring contributions from Richard Burton, Sergio Leone, Don Siegel, Pauline Kael and Dilys Powell and extracts from some of Eastwood's films, including Rawhide, The Good, the Bad and the Ugly, Where Eagles Dare, The Outlaw Josey Wales and The Enforcer.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah examines the skill of the shipbuilders and machine engineers who turned Britain into a great manufacturing nation. In Bristol, Fred visits the SS Great Britain and pays tribute to the designer and his hero, Isambard Kingdom Brunel. Fred also travels to Scotland to take a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley. Back in England, he visits the Windermere Steamboat Museum, the Long Shop Museum in Leiston, Suffolk, and the Kew Bridge Steam Museum. Plus, Fred drives his pride and joy, the Aveling & Porter steam roller, talking about its history and recalling one rather dramatic crash he had while driving it.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores London, the ultimate 'city of villages'. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expanding capital, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage. And in recent years, Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow.

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: As good as any Dickens novel, this is the triumphant and tragic story of the greatest architectural dynasty of the 19th century. Dan Cruickshank charts the rise of Sir George Gilbert Scott to the very heights of success, the fall of his son George Junior and the rise again of his grandson Giles. It is a story of architects bent on a mission to rebuild Britain. From the Romantic heights of the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station to the modern image of Bankside power station (now Tate Modern), this is the story of a family that shaped the Victorian age and left a giant legacy.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 10 September 1999 and featuring Martine McCutcheon, Shaft, Enrique Iglesias, Shania Twain, A1, The Offspring, DJ Jean and Lou Bega.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 17 September 1999 and featuring Supergrass, Sixpence None the Richer, Thunderbugs, The All Seeing I feat Phil Oakey, Lolly, Lyte Funkie Ones, Suede and Vengaboys.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long present another edition of the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 April 1983. Featuring Culture Club, The Human League, FR David, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 April 1994 and featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, C. J. Lewis, The Pretenders, Erasure, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue, Toni Di Bart and Prince.

programme.title: Prince and His Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of hits from one of pop music’s greatest geniuses - Prince, aka ‘The Artist Formerly Known as Prince’, aka ‘the Unpronounceable Symbol’, and more. Whichever alias he assumed, Prince was always fascinating as both a performer and songwriter, and here are the best of his own appearances on a selection of BBC shows, alongside a rich selection of performances from artists who covered his songs over the years. The playlist includes Beyonce, Muse, Tom Jones, The Bangles, Pink, Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka, Chaka Khan and, of course, Sinead O’Connor, all brought together to demonstrate beyond doubt why Nothing Compared 2 Him.

programme.title: Prince and The Revolution: Live
programme.desc: James Brown had the Apollo. Jimi Hendrix had Monterey Pop. And Prince had Syracuse, New York’s Carrier Dome – the site of his 30 March 1985 Purple Rain Tour performance that was beamed to millions live via satellite and captured for posterity as a Grammy-nominated concert film. For the first time, this powerful performance by Prince and The Revolution has been entirely remixed and remastered, and the picture quality has been improved significantly. The film features smash hits from the legendary Prince albums Purple Rain, Controversy and 1999, including a mind-bending 18-minute version of the song Purple Rain.

programme.title: New Power Generation: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Prince - showman, artist, enigma - revolutionised the perception of black music in the 1980s by embarking on an amazing journey of musical self-discovery.

programme.title: Word Up! Black American Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of some of the best performances by African-American artists of the 1980s from the BBC archives, featuring Cameo, Shalamar, Salt-n-Pepa, Chaka Khan, Kid Creole, Doug E Fresh, Whitney Houston and Luther Vandross.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Adrienne Murray visits the Esrange Spaceport in the extreme north of Sweden, to explore the latest tech being developed and tested inside Europe's space race. Shiona McCallum finds out more about Lego's new Smart Brick, and Alasdair Keane checks out the latest smart glasses. Plus, we hear from the visual effects team behind musical fantasy film Wicked: For Good.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Ronan Farrow, perhaps the world's most prominent investigative journalist, has turned his attention to Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI. The investigation, published by The New Yorker, draws on more than a year of reporting. Farrow outlines how it was conducted and the questions it raises about power and influence in AI. Madhumita Murgia, AI Editor at the Financial Times, gives her analysis of the issues at the heart of the report, including how the media covers leading figures in Silicon Valley. Also on the show, Misha Glenny discusses his podcast The Race to Control the World, as well as his role as the new presenter of In Our Time.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: With rising fuel prices and strangled shipping routes, Lukwesa Burak finds out how leaders of companies and countries alike are pivoting to cope with yet another game-changing economic crisis.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, farmer Mark Mahon is in despair as 12 of his sheep become stranded on rocks in Strangford Lough, one of the most dangerous tidal waters in the UK. With the tide rising fast and strong currents closing in, the RNLI and coastguard launch their most challenging rescue operation to date. In North Wales, a wildlife photographer captures evidence of a badger sett under attack. Quick thinking sets off an RSPCA and police investigation that uncovers a serious case of wildlife crime. And in South Yorkshire, reporter Paul Colgan joins the police Quad Squad as they tackle illegal off-road riding and the growing damage it causes to rural communities.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel is struck by the fetching colour of a County Durham house. Dion is impressed by what’s on offer in Greater Manchester and Derbyshire.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A sedan chair from the 1750s and a leather pigeon harness used to carry blood samples are among an intriguing collection that Danny Sebastian explores in Northamptonshire.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Carmarthen, friends Joy and Thembi hunt charity shop bargains, clash over risky buys and pin their hopes on bold upcycles in a tense, live online auction.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team head north to the Isle of Skye and their biggest transformation to date. Owners of a remote cafe and bookshop are desperate to update their business, but with a vast space to fill, Banjo takes the bold decision to spend almost all his budget on a set of huge stained-glass windows. Far from home, and with tricky logistics, will this job push the team to their limits, and will Banjo’s ambitious gamble pay off with his clients?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson joins the show's line-up as he helps a couple of born-and-bred Brummies to find the perfect character property on his home turf of the rural Midlands. A turnkey, ready-to-move-into home is high on the priority list for our busy buyers, one of whom is a primary school head teacher. But rebel Richie happily runs the risk of detention when he serves up a mystery house requiring a little extra love and attention. Continuing on his maiden voyage, Richie takes a break from property to skipper a narrowboat as he takes to the water in a bid to learn all about slow tourism.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom checks in on digital artist Tasia and pole lathe woodturner Shannon to hear about their stunning achievements and how much their skills are earning them after visiting his transformational business boot camp in the spring of 2022. Retail worker Tasia dreamt of becoming a full-time artist, and Dom finds out about her jaw-dropping success and the amount of money she is now making that is helping to turn that dream into a reality. Shannon reveals how broadening her business is helping her to build a long-term creative career and how much she is making from it.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Suraj, Natalie, Matty, Pearl and Cliff – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races, where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki starts to become paranoid, Priya continues to worry about Ravi, and Oscar puts Lauren and Max to the test.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team revive a powered football wheelchair, some meticulously handcrafted jewellery, a bagatelle board, and a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Ambulance returns to Yorkshire, following paramedics attending emergencies across the cities, towns and remote villages of England’s largest county.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Highlights from the quarter-final second legs in the UEFA Champions League. Premier League leaders Arsenal welcome Sporting CP to London and Bayern Munich face Real Madrid. There are also highlights from Tuesday’s matches, with Liverpool taking on the defending champions PSG and Athletico Madrid hosting Barcelona. Kelly Cates presents with analysis from Stephen Warnock and Nedum Onuoha.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, where in this edition, a team of athletes - Kriss Akabusi, Sammi Kinghorn, Matt Baker and Gladiator Karenjeet Kaur Bains - get together to take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Richard Ayoade, Alex Jones, Stephen Bailey, Nadia Jae, Gyles Brandreth and Roisin Conaty.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Nottinghamshire, police uncover shocking evidence of animal cruelty linked to a series of farmland poaching attacks. When officers raid a suspect’s property, they find disturbing scenes and uncover footage that leads to a painstaking investigation. With the help of estate managers, animal welfare investigators and neighbouring police forces, officers unite to bring animal cruelty offenders to justice. In Cumbria, a prized cow named Beauty becomes trapped at the bottom of a steep gorge, after a walker leaves a farm gate open. For her owner, Hunter Smith, the chances of survival look slim – until a daring rescue operation is launched, involving a local vet and the RAF. The team faces one of the most complex animal rescues ever attempted. And in Humberside, reporter Paul Colgan joins a rural crime officer using forensic DNA technology to mark valuable machinery – a simple but effective measure helping farmers protect their property and livelihoods.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion has to navigate his way through an eight bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui takes in the views in Dover. And Tommy is in Lincolnshire to look around a bungalow.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a special show, Eric Knowles celebrates the glamour and sophistication of the 1920s and 30s. He looks back at a host of art deco-inspired pieces bought by Bargain Hunt teams, from cocktail sets and mirrors to Anglepoise lamps and bronze sculptures. He also offers invaluable advice for collectors today. Eric admires the geometric architecture of the Jazz Age and visits Stockport’s stunning art deco cinema, as well as a unique home in Northampton designed by Charles Rennie Mackintosh.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Llandudno, Anne and Lenka race to turn charity shop bargains into profit, taking bold risks on furniture, jewellery and creative upcycles – all for charity.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo has two hospitality clients on the Isle of Mull, each with unique challenges. The B&B owners of the local castle want their unloved turret turned into a destination for guests, while the owner of a remote lighthouse wants to attract visitors by revamping a guest bedroom. Contending with tricky logistics and bad weather, Banjo and the team must go above and beyond to complete two makeovers in these stunning locations.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: North Yorkshire’s wide-open spaces are the ideal playground for a couple and their four children from a busy Hampshire town, who can’t wait to swap suburbia for a lifelong outdoorsy adventure. Briony May Williams is eager to help them find a family haven with room to roam. Her line-up of potential homes includes a gorgeous 18th-century former mill, complete with a dreamy country kitchen that might just tempt Briony to move in herself! Briony also meets another couple who have found their sweet spot in the county, turning their passion for bees into a buzzing new life.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom catches up with ceramic sculptor Sarah from Yorkshire and glass artist Emmy from Devon, both of whom attended his business boot camp in spring 2022, to find out how they have turned the advice they received into successful money-making ventures When Sarah arrived at the boot camp, she was new to her craft, but thanks to Dom and mentor Derek Wilson’s support and guidance, she has built a growing business. She tells Dom how she has done it and how much money her work is making her now. When we last saw Emmy, she was returning to glassblowing after an eight-year break. Now that she is selling around the world, Emma reveals exactly what that means for her bank balance.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Graeme, Anjy, Daniel, Mike and Emily – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki is out of options, and Max and Lauren continue to compete.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing toward the remote riverside town of Halfeti, where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It’s the final, and after 12 tough weeks, the last two candidates standing must go head-to-head in a battle to win Lord Sugar’s investment. Supported by returning candidates, the finalists must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry experts, as well as some legends from the last 20 years of the show. In the boardroom, Lord Sugar decides who will become his next business partner.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Police in Hertfordshire respond to an emergency that uncovers a shocking crime, Scottish fire crews battle a wildfire visible from space, and Paul Colgan joins farm safety training.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is dodging spiders at a house in Coventry. Owain is in Wales, where a detached house has a dodgy extension. In Bristol, Martin is swooning over a 1930s semi with bags of character.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Most people capture famous landmarks on their cameras, but Roo Irvine meets a man who makes them out of matchsticks! Michael Tolley measures the dimensions of Carlisle’s buildings by stepping them out. Then, he recreates them in miniature, using hundreds of matchsticks. So far, he has made 87 models, including a replica of an entire street. Over the border in Kelso, can the teams strike any good deals when they go bargain-hunting with experts David Harper and Irita Marriott? The blues are twin sisters Wendy and Gayle, although they weren’t born on the same day! Motorcycling fan Wendy is quick to spot a vintage oil can, while Gayle takes a shine to a white metal pin cushion. Over with the reds, friends Sue and (another) Wendy hope to get a pair of Victorian grape scissors for a snip. At Paul Laidlaw’s auction, the bids come in thick and fast, and at the end, one bonus buy sets hearts pounding.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown discovers there’s more drama offstage than on in the latest production by the Kembleford Players.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Bedfordshire beckons today’s couple, who are looking for the peace and quiet the county has to offer. Steve Brown guides them on their house hunt, and he also visits Biggleswade to find out about an easily accessible ‘off-road, multi-user path’ that circumnavigates the market town, enabling people to connect to the local countryside and its wildlife.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After giving them business-building action plans at his life-changing makers’ boot camp, Dom catches up with textile weaver Shiv from Brighton and portrait artist Jill from Berkshire to find out how they have turned them into the long-term, money-making careers they dreamt of. After seeing her at his boot camp in the summer of 2024, Dom drops in on Shiv at her studio to find out how her business has taken off and exactly what that means for her bottom line. In Berkshire, Jill reveals how her action plan led to her giving up her nursing job and how much she now earns from her art.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – George, Beth, Joyce, Matthew and Ria – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Jordan and Cian watched their mum lose her life at a young age to a rare form of dementia. Years later came another devastating blow as they found out they also carry the faulty gene, meaning they will almost certainly develop symptoms in their forties. Refusing to let the diagnosis define them, the brothers are determined to make their time count. Together, they began running for charity to raise awareness and funds for dementia research, turning fear and grief into purpose and action. Bill Bailey picks Unza Saleem to create a lasting legacy for the brothers, but as she is getting to grips with the portrait, she has her own family emergency which threatens to derail the project entirely.

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When a seaweed farm is targeted in an act of sabotage, all the evidence points to the legendary mermaid of Shipton Abbott.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Gabby Logan hosts, with guest panellists comedians Miles Jupp and Michelle Wolf joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Linda Walsh, Conor's one true love, is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award-winning show Schitt's Creek, who discusses his new venture, Netflix's Big Mistakes; actor Phil Dunster, who shot to fame in the hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new series, Rooster, on HBO Max; star of stage and screen Cush Jumbo, who chats about the return of the second season of her British crime thriller Criminal Record; and comedian Josh Widdicombe, who discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture.

programme.title: The Royal Hotel
programme.desc: Broke backpackers Hanna and Liv, working in a remote mining community’s run-down pub, are seen as fresh meat by the mainly male punters, and the boundaries of acceptable behaviour soon begin to blur.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out and how prison changed her for the better.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James attempt to save the planet by building their own electric car and then put their latest 'green' creation through the rigours of the new car testing process. Meanwhile on the track, it is the battle of the continents as Europe takes on the USA and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1's muscle against the finesse of Audi's R8 V10. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Frost/Nixon and The Damned United star Michael Sheen.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch comedy set on a seemingly quiet residential street where the neighbours are all just trying to get through the day without incident. But beneath the calm exterior, everyday life quickly unravels into the absurd, the awkward and the unexpectedly hilarious - from dodging a secret crush to desperately trying to impress the builder. Whether it’s a piano teacher adapting to a four-legged student, a cycling enthusiast battling to stay inside his own outfit, or a work-from-home mum constantly thrown off course, life on the street is anything but peaceful. Blending sharp observational humour with bursts of slapstick chaos, the show transforms suburban routine into a playground of comic mayhem - where absolutely anything can happen.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Los Angeles is home to more than a quarter of a million dogs - from the pampered pooches of Beverley Hills to the pit bull terriers of the projects. Louis Theroux enters the bizarre world of LA's dogs, meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the city's huge canine population. In the toughest neighbourhoods of south LA, he has a terrifying encounter with a 'weaponised' dog, trained to attack on command, and goes on patrol with 'Dogman', whose mission is to rescue some of the thousands of stray and feral pit bulls roaming the streets. At the city's biggest animal shelter, Louis meets the hard-pressed staff, whose heartbreaking job includes euthanizing thousands of unwanted dogs every year. He also encounters the middle-class Americans who adopt some of these delinquent dogs, as well as the colourful dog-training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once the dog has been rescued from the streets.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew continue their search for the planet's deadliest predators with a visit to Florida in the USA. Steve wades into a swamp to try and catch a slippery amphibian called the amphiuma and tracks down America's largest snake, the eastern diamondback rattlesnake. His journey ends in the Florida Keys where he witnesses firsthand the sophisticated hunting methods of highly intelligent bottlenose dolphins.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this special episode Steve Backshall reveals the advanced camera technology and tricks of the trade that allow him and the Deadly 60 crew to film stunning images of the planet's most lethal predators. Including the high-speed camera that captured the breathtaking breach attack of the great white shark and the specially designed camera traps that let the crew film wildlife when they're not even there.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring news and views with Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May. Jeremy and Richard settle the thorny issue of which company has, above all others, made the greatest number of truly brilliant cars. James has a novel solution for getting caravans to campsites without clogging up the roads, and Jeremy hits the track in a remarkable replica of a legendary rally car. Plus Radio 2's Breakfast Show DJ and all round petrolhead Chris Evans is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It's week 11, and the final five candidates must face a gruelling round of interviews with some of Lord Sugar’s toughest-talking business associates. As their business plans are picked apart, some candidates hold their own, while others crumble under the pressure. In the boardroom, Lord Sugar has a tough decision to make, as only two can make it through to next week’s grand final.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Brazil’s favelas are considered extremely dangerous, making them one of the world’s most deadly places to grow up. As a result of poverty and a lack of government and police control, many young men here resort to drug dealing and gang crime under the control of the drug cartels. In other neighbourhoods, heavily armed criminal militia groups control day-to-day life with an iron grip, extorting residents – fail to pay or cross them, and the consequences can be deadly. Ashley meets young men living in some of Rio’s most notorious favelas to find out what it’s like to live here and what forces them to join gangs to survive. He will learn what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become a reality at any moment. And he’ll come face to face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule the favelas through fear and intimidation.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these brutal acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead to illegal poaching. Hanging out with men from KwaZulu-Natal – or Zululand – he earns their trust and joins them on a legal hunt for bushmeat in order to understand what day-to-day life is like here. He immerses himself in all sides of the issue - meeting an illegal poacher who is horrifyingly responsible for over 150 rhino deaths and a family whose son was killed while attempting to hunt a rhino. He even joins antipoaching units to see the measures being used to protect these incredible creatures.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Marseille has been France’s drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of the city's drug war is escalating as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the 'cités', meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war. He also rides along with the police on a night-time patrol. To understand the present, Ashley needs to learn of Marseille’s past, so he meets a former gangster who was part of the city’s infamous French Connection drugs trade in the 1970s and 80s. Ashley also meets people trying to steer young men away from gang life, video calls a gang boss behind bars and meets a small-time dealer who became a deadly hitman.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. She goes to a high-octane pool party to discover how casinos are cashing in on the party scene.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Four very different schoolgirls are thrown together in detention. As they plan their escape, the situation spirals out of control.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: When a teenage boy has a girl over for the first time, the overly attached monster under his bed has a meltdown. A comedy short film from Uncle Shortbread.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steve’s daughter. If they fail, Steve’s in the doghouse and Stu might end up... dead.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sitcom set in 1990s east London, following the lives of the chaotic Potts family. Joyce Potts’s birthday present has been stolen, so her husband Big Jim enlists the help of his fearsome mother-in-law Edna to track it down on the Thames View Estate.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast from Northern Irish creator rarewitch. The cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical, and Gambo forgets how to breathe. Surreal, chaotic and hilariously absurd – welcome to the madcap world of Glue.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: In the final episode of Louis Theroux's LA Stories, Louis spends time in the twilight world of the city's paroled sex offenders, whose crimes have made them pariahs living on the fringes of society. The State of California has some of the toughest regulations anywhere in America controlling sex offenders. On leaving prison they can spend years on parole, tagged and tracked, and a lifetime on an online sex offenders register. Louis spent several months at some of the hostels in Los Angeles where sex offenders live, talking in depth to sex offenders themselves as well as the people charged with monitoring them. The tough rules governing where they can live and who they can see make it hard for registered sex offenders to find work or even housing, and many are forced into homelessness. Meeting these outcasts Louis asks whether these tough rules keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is caught in a fight to the death as a spaceship crash-lands in London. But as the battle wreaks havoc, destiny is heading for the Doctor’s old friend, Donna.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Tardis takes the Doctor and Donna to the furthest edge of adventure. To escape, they must face the most desperate fight of their lives, with the fate of the universe at stake.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The giggle of a mysterious puppet is driving the human race insane. When the Doctor discovers the return of the terrifying Toymaker, he faces a fight he can never win.

programme.title: Talking Doctor Who
programme.desc: As part of the sixtieth anniversary celebrations of Doctor Who, David Tennant time-travels back through the BBC archives to tell the story of the Doctor’s classic era, with a selection of rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnell’s first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy. On the way, David compares some of his own experiences with the actors who came before him and shares special archive moments that reveal, amongst other things, how to film a regeneration, which actor was a master of modern gadgetry - including a collapsible caravan - and what the factors were that helped to decide how a Doctor selects his own individual and distinctive costume.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Steffan Powell catches up with David Tennant and Catherine Tate as they film Doctor Who: The Star Beast. David gives us a tour of the Tardis, and we meet Yasmin Finney, who plays Rose, for the first time. We also welcome the original comic book creators Pat Mills and Dave Gibbons on set, where they meet the Meep for the first time. Stay tuned for a sneak peek of the second sixtieth anniversary special, Wild Blue Yonder.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year, but that doesn't stop David Tennant, Catherine Tate and the crew from making Doctor Who. Join Steffan Powell as he witnesses David and Catherine getting to grips with their prosthetics, and he finds out how to get a job in the camera department!

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Steffan Powell goes behind the scenes of The Giggle and speaks to the Toymaker, Neil Patrick Harris. We meet the fifteenth Doctor, Ncuti Gatwa, and find out what it's like to become the Doctor for the first time.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring news and views with Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May. Jeremy and Richard settle the thorny issue of which company has, above all others, made the greatest number of truly brilliant cars. programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

On the way, David compares some of his own experiences with the actors who came before him and shares special archive moments that reveal, amongst other things, how to film a regeneration, which actor was a master of modern gadgetry - including a collapsible caravan - and what the factors were that helped to decide how a Doctor selects his own individual and distinctive costume.
